factors.summary: The company is implementing cost efficiency measures, leading to improvements in selling and administrative expenses as a percentage of revenue.

evidence.text: Selling and administrative expenses for the Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment increased $51.5 million improving as a percent to revenue... The improvements in percent of revenue was primarily due to efficiencies in labor and the previously mentioned one-time gain... Selling and administrative expenses increased $11.2 million, and increased as a percent of revenue to 32.1%,... The increase in expenses as a percent of revenue was primarily due to an investment in the sales force to support our strong current fiscal year revenue growth as well as anticipated future revenue growth through the remainder of the fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is not material, as per management's assessment.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to other legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business... In the opinion of management, the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect on the consolidated financial position, consolidated results of operations or consolidated cash flows of Cintas.
evidence.subsection: Litigation and Other Contingencies

factors.summary: Demand is improving as customer activity and business operations are recovering from the impact of COVID-19.

evidence.text: The on-going roll out of the COVID-19 vaccines and gradual lifting of COVID-19 restrictions had a positive impact on our business during the three and nine months ended February 28, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, supported by strong customer demand and new business.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business. New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, improving overall profitability.

evidence.text: The improvement in cost of sales as a percent to revenue was primarily due to favorable changes in the sales mix for each of the underlying operating segments, including a decrease in the proportion of sales related to personal protective equipment, which typically have lower gross margins compared to the first aid cabinet sales in the First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased sales and customer activity.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business. New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: End markets, particularly those related to facility services and safety, are growing fastest, supported by increased demand and customer activity.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business. New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with increases offset by revenue growth and operational efficiencies.

evidence.text: The increase in expense was primarily due to increases in selling labor and increased travel and meeting expenses. Selling and administrative expenses as a percent of revenue were 25.0% for the three months ended February 28, 2022, which is a 220 basis point improvement compared to 27.2% for the same period in the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are favorable, with the company gaining market share through product differentiation and customer service.

evidence.text: Cintas is also the creator of the Total Clean Program ™ — a first-of-its-kind service that includes scheduled delivery of essential cleaning supplies, hygienically clean laundering, and sanitizing and disinfecting products and services.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technology, scale, and customer relationships, which are strengthening.

evidence.text: Cintas is also the creator of the Total Clean Program ™ — a first-of-its-kind service that includes scheduled delivery of essential cleaning supplies, hygienically clean laundering, and sanitizing and disinfecting products and services.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving, reducing reliance on a few large customers.

evidence.text: Cintas helps more than one million businesses of all types and sizes, primarily in the United States (U.S.), as well as Canada and Latin America, get READY ™ to open their doors with confidence every day by providing a wide range of products and services that enhance our customers’ image and help keep their facilities and employees clean, safe and looking their best.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: Management is maintaining forward guidance, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: The company is on track to meet its financial targets, reflecting management's confidence in the future.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by the company's strategic initiatives and financial performance.

evidence.text: Cintas’ principal objective is “to exceed customers’ expectations in order to maximize the long-term value of Cintas for shareholders and working partners,” and it provides the framework and focus for Cintas’ business strategy.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential disruptions from the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: The impact of the on-going COVID-19 pandemic, including the emergence of the Omicron variant, is fluid and continues to evolve, and therefore, we cannot predict the extent to which our business, consolidated results of operations, consolidated financial condition or liquidity will ultimately be impacted.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding product offerings and geographic reach.

evidence.text: We will also continue to identify additional product and service opportunities for our current and future customers. We pursue the strategy of broadening our customer base in several ways.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, reducing debt and refinancing at lower rates.

evidence.text: Net interest expense (interest expense less interest income) was $22.0 million for the three months ended February 28, 2022, compared to $24.5 million for the three months ended February 28, 2021. The change was primarily due to the replacement of the $250.0 million of senior notes with an interest rate of 4.30% that matured on June 1, 2021, with commercial paper that had an interest rate of 0.43% at February 28, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are having a neutral to positive impact, supporting revenue growth.

evidence.text: Total organic revenue growth was 9.3%. Organic growth adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are a potential concern, particularly in global supply chains and trade relations.

evidence.text: We have operations throughout the U.S. and Canada and participate in a global supply chain.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditures have increased, reflecting a commitment to growth opportunities. However, the evidence also suggests that capital expenditures are maintained, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $165.9 million and $100.4 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2022 and 2021, respectively. The increase in capital expenditures during the nine months ended February 28, 2022, over the same period in the prior year is due to an investment in the operating segments to support the on-going growth in revenue.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: The company is investing in its infrastructure and technology to support growth.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, supported by strong operating cash flows and capital expenditures, as well as revenue growth and cost management.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $987.1 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2022, compared to $904.8 million... Cash flows provided by operating activities have historically supplied us with a significant source of liquidity... We expect our cash flows from operating activities to remain sufficient to provide us with adequate levels of liquidity.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: Net income for the three months ended February 28, 2022, increased $57.1 million, or 22.1%, compared to the three months ended February 28, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The company is demonstrating strong organic revenue growth across its reportable segments, with acceleration noted in recent periods. The growth is driven by new business, product penetration, and price increases, though some segments experienced slower growth compared to others.

evidence.text: Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment revenue increased 8.9%... Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 8.5%. First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased from $597.4 million to $614.2 million, or 2.8%,... Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 2.0%
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2021

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth rate for the reportable operating segment was 8.9%. Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The company's margins show a mixed trend, with gross margins improving slightly, but operating margins showing a more significant expansion. The improvement in operating margins is attributed to cost efficiencies and higher income percentages, while gross margins have seen a slight decline.

evidence.text: The reportable operating segment’s gross margin was $2,166.1 million, or 47.1% of revenue, for the nine months ended February 28, 2022, compared to the gross margin of 47.5%... The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 44.2% for the nine months ended February 28, 2022, which was an increase of 200 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 42.2% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2021

evidence.text: Operating income was $407.6 million, or 20.8% of revenue, for the three months ended February 28, 2022, compared to $326.5 million, or 18.4% of revenue, for the three months ended February 28, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The economic conditions are favorable, supported by strong cash flows, liquidity, and the ongoing recovery from the pandemic. Access to significant debt capacity further enhances the company's financial stability.

evidence.text: Cash flows provided by operating activities have historically supplied us with a significant source of liquidity... We expect our cash flows from operating activities to remain sufficient to provide us with adequate levels of liquidity. In addition, we have access to $2.0 billion of debt capacity from our recently amended and restated revolving credit facility, the maturity of which was extended.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: The on-going roll out of the COVID-19 vaccines and gradual lifting of COVID-19 restrictions had a positive impact on our business during the three and nine months ended February 28, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized and increased through active share buyback programs and dividend payments, reflecting management confidence. The company has demonstrated a commitment to returning value to shareholders by purchasing a significant number of shares, though dividend decisions remain discretionary.

evidence.text: From the inception of the July 27, 2021 share buyback program through April 7, 2022, Cintas has purchased 1.5 million shares of Cintas common stock in the aggregate...
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: Any future dividend declarations, including the amount of any dividends, are at the discretion of the Board of Directors and dependent upon then-existing conditions...
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

evidence.text: The decrease in diluted weighted average common shares outstanding resulted from purchasing an aggregate of approximately 4.4 million shares of common stock under the board approved share buyback programs since the beginning of the third quarter of fiscal 2021 through the third quarter of fiscal 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The company is actively engaging in acquisition activity, which has resulted in significant cash outflows and a positive impact on revenue growth.

evidence.text: Cash paid for acquisitions of businesses was $150.8 million and $7.6 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2022 and 2021, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: Revenue growth was positively impacted by a net 0.3% due to acquisitions and divestitures.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly energy, are rising, creating margin pressure but partially offset by pricing or productivity gains. The company is managing these rising costs through operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The change in gross margin was primarily due to a 50 basis point increase in energy costs in the current year.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2021

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 44.2% for the nine months ended February 28, 2022, which was an increase of 200 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 42.2% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

evidence.text: This change from the prior fiscal year was primarily due to higher Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment sales volume, as well as increased energy costs and labor to generate the revenue growth achieved during the three months ended February 28, 2022 as well as anticipated revenue growth during the remainder of the current fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021

factors.summary: The company is effectively managing interest rate changes, benefiting from lower borrowing costs and increased access to capital markets.

evidence.text: The credit agreement that supports our commercial paper program was amended and restated on March 23, 2022. The amendment increased the capacity of the revolving credit facility from $1.0 billion to $2.0 billion... As of February 28, 2022, there was $559.2 million of commercial paper outstanding with a weighted average interest rate of 0.43% and maturity dates less than 120 days and no borrowings on our revolving credit facility.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: Net interest expense (interest expense less interest income) was $22.0 million for the three months ended February 28, 2022, compared to $24.5 million for the three months ended February 28, 2021. The change was primarily due to the replacement of the $250.0 million of senior notes with an interest rate of 4.30% that matured on June 1, 2021, with commercial paper that had an interest rate of 0.43% at February 28, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2021
